@@ -36,17 +36,11 @@ to select targets.
 
 ### Install individual skills
 
-Use the `--skill` flag to install specific skills:
+Append `@<skill-name>` to install a specific skill:
 
 ```bash
-npx skills add tenzir/skills --skill ocsf
-npx skills add tenzir/skills --skill tenzir-docs
-```
-
-You can also combine multiple skills in one command:
-
-```bash
-npx skills add tenzir/skills --skill ocsf --skill tenzir-docs
+npx skills add tenzir/skills@ocsf
+npx skills add tenzir/skills@tenzir-docs
 ```
 
 ### Choose the installation scope
@@ -67,7 +61,7 @@ npx skills add -g tenzir/skills
 Install a specific skill globally:
 
 ```bash
-npx skills add -g tenzir/skills --skill ocsf
+npx skills add -g tenzir/skills@ocsf
 ```
 
 ### Target specific agents

@@ -1,94 +0,0 @@
----
-title: Create an OCSF mapping
----
-
-import WorkflowExecutor from '@partials/workflows/WorkflowExecutor.mdx';
-import Guide from '@components/see-also/Guide.astro';
-import Op from '@components/see-also/Op.astro';
-import Reference from '@components/see-also/Reference.astro';
-import Tutorial from '@components/see-also/Tutorial.astro';
-
-This workflow guides you through creating an OCSF mapping for a parser package.
-It builds on a [parser package](/reference/workflows/create-parser-package),
-adding OCSF-specific mapping and validation steps.
-
-<WorkflowExecutor />
-
-## 1. Create a parser package
-
-Before mapping to OCSF, you need a package that can parse and clean your source
-data. Complete a parser package workflow first.
-
-**Results**:
-
-- A working parser package
-- All parsing tests passing
-
-**Resources**:
-
-- <Reference>workflows/create-parser-package</Reference>
-
-## 2. Identify the OCSF event class
-
-Analyze your source events to determine which OCSF event class best represents
-them. Consider the event semantics, not just field names.
-
-**Results**:
-
-- The OCSF event class (e.g., `network_activity`, `authentication`)
-- Understanding of required vs. optional attributes
-
-**Resources**:
-
-- [OCSF schema](https://schema.ocsf.io)
-- <Guide>normalization/map-to-ocsf</Guide>
-
-## 3. Write mapping operators
-
-For each log type, create a user-defined operator that transforms cleaned events
-into the OCSF schema. Map source fields to OCSF attributes, handling type
-conversions and nested structures.
-
-**Results**:
-
-- Main dispatching operator: `acme::vendor::product::ocsf::map`
-- Test: `acme/tests/product/ocsf/map.{tql,txt}`
-- Event-specific operator(s): `acme::vendor::product::ocsf::event::foo`
-- Test(s): `acme/tests/product/ocsf/event/log.{tql,txt}`
-
-**Resources**:
-
-- <Guide>normalization/map-to-ocsf</Guide>
-- <Tutorial>learn-idiomatic-tql</Tutorial>
-
-## 4. Add end-to-end examples
-
-Add examples showing the complete pipeline from raw data to OCSF output:
-
-```tql
-from_* {
-  acme::product::parse
-  acme::product::clean
-  acme::product::ocsf::map
-}
-```
-
-**Results**:
-
-- Examples demonstrating the full normalization pipeline
-
-**Resources**:
-
-- <Guide>collecting</Guide>
-
-## 5. Finalize package
-
-Update the package manifest to include the OCSF mapping operator. Review
-operators for idiomatic TQL patterns. Verify all tests pass, including OCSF
-validation.
-
-**Results**:
-
-- TQL code adheres to idiomatic best practices
-- All tests pass
-- Updated package manifest with OCSF operator documentation

@@ -1,116 +0,0 @@
----
-title: Create a parser package
----
-
-import WorkflowExecutor from '@partials/workflows/WorkflowExecutor.mdx';
-import Guide from '@components/see-also/Guide.astro';
-import Tutorial from '@components/see-also/Tutorial.astro';
-
-This workflow guides you through creating a package to parse and clean log
-events. To this end, you scaffold a Tenzir package and build user-defined
-operators.
-
-<WorkflowExecutor />
-
-## 1. Understand your data
-
-Start with a deep understanding of the sample data given.
-
-**Results**:
-
-- Context around the given samples
-- Name of the vendor and product for the package
-
-**Resources**:
-
-- Internet: search for extra samples to better understand product and vendor.
-
-## 2. Create the package scaffold
-
-Set up the directory structure and manifest file for your package.
-
-**Results**:
-
-- A package for vendor `acme`
-- A package manifest: `acme/package.yaml`
-- Event samples in `acme/tests/inputs`
-
-**Resources**:
-
-- <Guide>packages/create-a-package</Guide>
-
-## 3. Add operators
-
-Create user-defined operators for parsing and cleaning. Use the testing
-framework to validate each step of work.
-
-**Resources**:
-
-- <Guide>packages/add-operators</Guide>
-- <Guide>packages/test-packages</Guide>
-
-### 3.1 Parsing operator
-
-The parsing operator translates unstructured into structured data. This is only
-needed when the data is not yet parsed, i.e., a raw string, or when it contains
-fields that are not yet parsed, e.g., a JSON with `payload` field.
-
-**Results**:
-
-- Operator: `acme::product::parse`
-- Test(s): `acme/tests/product/parse.{tql,txt}`
-
-**Resources**:
-
-- <Guide>parsing/parse-delimited-text</Guide>
-- <Guide>parsing/parse-string-fields</Guide>
-- <Tutorial>learn-idiomatic-tql</Tutorial>
-
-### 3.2 Cleaning operator
-
-The cleaning operator ensures semantically rich types, replaces sentinel values,
-and ensures a proper schema if feasible.
-
-**Results**:
-
-- Operator: `acme::product::clean`
-- Test(s): `acme/tests/product/clean.{tql,txt}`
-
-**Resources**:
-
-- <Guide>normalization/clean-up-values</Guide>
-- <Guide>transformation/work-with-time</Guide>
-- <Tutorial>learn-idiomatic-tql</Tutorial>
-
-## 4. Add examples
-
-Add end-to-end examples that showcase how to use the operators. These examples
-should reflect the collection method that is typical for the given
-vendor-product combination, e.g.,
-
-```tql
-from_* {
-  acme::product::parse
-  acme::product::clean
-}
-```
-
-**Results**:
-
-- A few examples that show how to use the operators in the package
-
-**Resources**:
-
-- <Guide>collecting</Guide>
-
-## 5. Finalize package
-
-Update the package manifest with accurate descriptions reflecting the final
-implementation. Review operators for idiomatic TQL patterns. Verify all tests
-pass.
-
-**Results**:
-
-- TQL code adheres to idiomatic best practices
-- All tests pass
-- Updated and descriptive package manifest

@@ -1,16 +0,0 @@
-import { Tabs, TabItem } from '@astrojs/starlight/components';
-
-<Tabs>
-  <TabItem label="Human" icon="pencil">
-    Complete each numbered step in order. Each step includes:
-    - **Results**: What you should have when done
-    - **Resources**: Guides and references to help you
-  </TabItem>
-  <TabItem label="AI Agent" icon="star">
-    Execute this workflow systematically:
-    1. Enter plan mode to outline all steps
-    2. Create a tasklist when leaving plan mode
-    3. Execute each step in a subagent, verifying **Results** before proceeding
-    4. Use **Resources** links for implementation details
-  </TabItem>
-</Tabs>
